stories.title: Global oil and energy prices are reeling as the war in the Middle East escalates
stories.description: Israel and Iran continue to exchange airstrikes with a focus on energy infrastructure. President Trump says Israel acted alone in striking a key gas field and better not do it again.

stories.title: Israel launches more strikes on Tehran as Iran continues attacks on Gulf oil facilities
stories.description: The latest strikes come after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Thursday that Israel would "hold off on future attacks" on Iran's energy infrastructure, following Trump's request.

stories.title: Why it's so hard for world leaders to bring down oil and gasoline prices
stories.description: From waiving the Jones Act to rerouting oil through the Red Sea, governments are doing their best to make up for the crisis in the Strait of Hormuz, but prices are still rising.

stories.title: Morning news brief
stories.description: Global oil and energy prices reel as the war in the Middle East escalates, Trump talks about potential for ground troops in Iran and the possibility of seizing Kharg Island.

stories.title: Netanyahu says Israel 'acted alone' in attack on Iranian gas field
stories.description: Speaking at a news conference, the Israeli leader said Trump had requested that there be no further such attacks.

stories.title: 'You can't smell Nowruz in the air': Iran marks Persian new year under threat of strikes
stories.description: Iranians speak to the BBC about preparing for the Nowruz new year festival during the war.

stories.title: Are US and Israel aligned on Iran war? Deciphering Trump's post after gas field attacks
stories.description: What does Trump's Truth Social post after gas field attacks tell us about US-Israeli alignment?

stories.title: Teenager among first to be executed over Iran's anti-government protests
stories.description: Saleh Mohammadi, a member of Iran's national wrestling team, was found guilty of killing police officers alongside two other men, state media say.

stories.title: Three Palestinian women killed in beauty salon during Iranian missile attack
stories.description: Others were injured when the salon in the southern West Bank was hit, while a Thai worker was killed by falling shrapnel in Israel.

stories.title: 'Very difficult to stop': BBC visits scene of Iran cluster bomb strike on Israel
stories.description: An elderly couple was killed after a bomb flew into their apartment in central Israel and exploded.

stories.title: Why the Strait of Hormuz matters so much in the Iran war
stories.description: Iran says it will "set fire" to ships trying to sail through the world's most vital oil transit point.

stories.title: Iran's intelligence minister Esmail Khatib killed in air strike
stories.description: Israel said it had killed Khatib, whose death was confirmed by Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday.

stories.title: Surge in US gas prices deepens political peril for Trump over Iran
stories.description: The odds of a quick resolution to the war appear to be dwindling by the day, and the political stakes for the president are only growing.

stories.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
stories.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

stories.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
stories.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: How Iranians are evading internet blocks to contact family abroad
stories.description: Iranians are finding tech-savvy ways to get through the regime's restrictions on phone and internet connections.

stories.title: Under drone fire, exiled Kurds wait to confront Iranian regime
stories.description: A group of fighters based in Iraq say they are "ready to go home", as they face drone attacks from Tehran.

stories.title: As hopes of regime change in Iran fade, Netanyahu faces political test
stories.description: Israeli leaders are framing the bombing campaign as having transformed the Middle East in their favour as pressure to end the conflict builds.

stories.title: 'Online diplomacy': How Trump narrates war in Iran on Truth Social
stories.description: PRESS REVIEW – Friday, March 20: Donald Trump is turning to "online diplomacy" to defend his actions in Iran and lambast his critics on Truth Social. The Times of London looks at how Monica Witt, a US servicewoman-turned-spy for Iran, could play a crucial role in the war. Also: a...

stories.title: Trump compares US strikes on Iran to WWII Pearl Harbor attack
stories.description: During Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's visit to Washington, Donald #Trump was asked why #US allies - including #Japan - were not involved in talks regarding strikes in #Iran before they happened. Trump said the reason was surprise, before asking why Japan hadn’t warned o...

stories.title: Iran hits Kuwaiti oil refinery as attacks on oil infrastructure continue
stories.description: Firefighters battled a blaze at a giant oil refinery in Kuwait on Friday after a fresh drone attack as Iran kept up a wave of strikes on its neighbours as it continues to target energy infrastructure in the Gulf region. France 24's regional correspondent Hoda Abdel-Hamid reports ...

stories.title: In pictures: Muslims celebrate Eid al-Fitr across the world in shadow of Middle East war
stories.description: Muslims on Friday gathered to celebrate the Eid al-Fitr festival that marks the end of the holy month of Ramadan. FRANCE 24 looks at how communities across the world are coming together in prayer and celebration even as the US-Israeli war on Iran casts its shadow over the Middle ...

stories.title: Netanyahu says Iran 'decimated,' Tehran claims only used ‘fraction’ of firepower
stories.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Thursday that Israel and the United States were "winning" the war against Iran, with the Islamic republic "decimated" and unable to enrich uranium or manufacture ballistic missiles. However, Tehran is pushing back in this escalating war of w...

stories.title: 'There's a lot of ways that this could escalate and get worse': Iran war rattles energy markets
stories.description: Dan Marks, a research fellow in energy security at the Royal United Services Institute, breaks down the impact of Israel's attack on Iran's massive South Pars natural gas field on global energy markets – and just how both sides could escalate the crisis with further attacks on cr...

stories.title: Middle East war live: US-Israeli air strikes kill Iran’s Revolutionary Guard spokesperson
stories.description: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps spokesperson Ali Mohammad Naini was killed in dawn US-Israeli air strikes on Friday, state media reported. The strikes came as an Iranian drone attack hit Kuwait’s Mina al-Ahmadi oil refinery, causing several fires but no casualties. Follo...

stories.title: US reportedly weighing military reinforcements as Iran war enters possible new phase
stories.description: President Donald Trump's administration is considering deploying thousands of U.S. troops to reinforce its operation in the Middle East, as the U.S. military prepares for ​possible next steps in its campaign against Iran, Reuters reports quoting a U.S. official and three people f...

stories.title: Video of Iranian soldier being targeted in Tehran strike is AI-generated
stories.description: Since Tuesday, videos supposedly showing Iranian soldiers being bombed in Tehran have been widely shared by pro-Israel accounts, Israeli media and even an official Israeli government social media account. But doubts have surfaced over their authenticity, as Emerald Maxwell explai...

stories.title: Netanyahu seeks to appease Trump with latest Iran remarks to the press
stories.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday denied what he called "fake news" that Israel had dragged US President Donald Trump into war with Iran. "Does anyone really think that someone can tell President Trump what to do?" Netanyahu told journalist at a press conferen...

stories.title: Refugee Council questions Australia’s Iran strategy at Women’s Asian Cup
stories.description: Women's Asian Cup hosts Australia face criticism for the political tug of war over the Iranian women's football team.

stories.title: Iran today, Africa tomorrow
stories.description: What is being done to Iran today could be done to Africa tomorrow. Africa must reject this illegal war.

stories.title: Iran does not need to close the Strait of Hormuz to disrupt it
stories.description: The real risk lies not in closure, but in disruption in the strait’s approaches.

stories.title: Iran developing a ‘vetting system’ for Strait of Hormuz transit: Report
stories.description: Lloyd's List reports that ships could be allowed to pass through 'safe corridor' once they are approved by IRGC.

stories.title: Eid celebrations dimmed by war and displacement across Middle East
stories.description: Economic crises and conflict dampen traditional Eid celebrations in Iran, Gaza, and Lebanon.

stories.title: Five problems the Iran war could solve for Israel’s Netanyahu
stories.description: From the Iranian threat to his own domestic legal and political troubles, war with Iran has advantages for Netanyahu.

stories.title: Iran hits Kuwaiti oil refinery as Israel renews attacks on Tehran
stories.description: Attacks stretch from Kuwait to the UAE as analysts warn conflict is entering a dangerous new phase.

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 21 of US-Israel attacks?
stories.description: Tehran has warned of zero restraint if energy facilities are attacked again, while Netanyahu points to ground operation.

stories.title: Iran women’s football team feted in Tehran after asylum battle at Asian Cup
stories.description: Iran’s national football team returned to their war-torn nation after several of the players sought asylum in Australia.

stories.title: Iranian football players celebrated in Tehran
stories.description: Iran’s national women’s football team has been given a hero’s welcome home, weeks after they were thrust into a crisis.

stories.title: Iran footballer Azmoun kicked off national team for disloyalty, say reports
stories.description: Reports say Sardar Azmoun, who plays for UAE club Shabab Al-Ahli, was expelled for Instagram post with Dubai's ‌ruler.

stories.title: Unpacking Netanyahu’s latest claims about the war on Iran
stories.description: Benjamin Netanyahu claimed to have made significant progress in 20 days of war on Iran but indicated more is to come.

stories.title: Trump must ‘take the steering wheel back from the Israelis’ in Iran war
stories.description: Trita Parsi, argues that US President Trump must not allow Israel to control the war with Iran.

stories.title: Palestinians bury four women killed in Iranian missile attack
stories.description: A small occupied West Bank community has buried four women killed in a hair salon during an Iranian missile attack.

stories.title: Jerusalem’s Muslims despair on Eidul Fitr as access to Al-Aqsa Mosque denied amid US-Israeli war on Iran
stories.description: Hundreds of Muslim worshippers held Eidul Fitr prayers at the gates of Jerusalem’s Old City on Friday, with Israel closing access to the Al-Aqsa mosque and other holy sites over its war on Iran. “Today, Al-Aqsa has been taken from us. It’s a sad and painful Ramazan,” Wajdi Mohamm...

stories.description: SMOKE rises after an Iranian retaliatory salvo damaged an Israeli oil refinery in Haifa.—Reuters • Tehran strikes Qatari LNG plant, Saudi and Kuwaiti refineries• Trump warns of ‘furious response’ if attacks on Qatar continue• Rules out troop deployment, but officials say reinforc...

stories.title: Hezbollah battles Israeli troops in Lebanon
stories.description: BEIRUT: Hezbollah said on Thursday its fighters were battling Israeli forces in south Lebanon, as a military source on the ground said Israeli troops were slowly advancing while “systematically destroying” border towns. Lebanon was drawn into the Middle East war on March 2 when H...

stories.title: Japan PM Takaichi placates Trump on Iran, but faces Pearl Harbor surprise
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi sailed smoothly past a potential clash with Donald Trump on Iran on Thursday — although the US president whipped up a bit of a storm about Pearl Harbor. Days after lashing out at US allies including Japan for failing to heed his ...

stories.title: Israel kills four in Gaza as Rafah reopens
stories.description: MINIBUSES carrying Palestinians to the Gaza Strip, line up on the Egyptian side of the Rafah crossing.—AFP • Tel Aviv bombs Tuffah and Zeitoun neighbourhoods, local officials say• Some wounded Palestinians cross Gaza-Egypt border amid fresh truce push GAZA CITY: Israeli airstrike...

stories.description: United Nations Secretary General Antonio Guterres on Thursday called on the US and Isreal to end the war on Iran while urging Tehran to stop “attacking your neighbours”. “Let me start by two clear messages. stories.description: Leaders from the United Kingdom, France, Germany, Italy, the Netherlands, and Japan have signalled their readiness to support efforts to ensure the safe passage of commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz. “We express our readiness to contribute to appropriate efforts to e...

stories.description: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) claimed on Thursday that it had struck and “seriously damaged” a US F-35 fighter jet over central Iran. stories.title: IMF raises concern over global inflation, output over US-Israel war on Iran

stories.title: Ministerial meeting calls on Iran to ‘immediately and unconditionally’ halt attacks in Gulf
stories.description: A ministerial meeting, hosted by Saudi Arabia, called on Iran to “immediately and unconditionally” halt attacks against Gulf countries in the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, the Saudi Arabian Foreign Ministry said on Thursday. stories.title: ‘Baseless characterisation’: FO rejects remarks by Israel’s ambassador to India
stories.description: The Foreign Office (FO) on Thursday issued a statement rejecting the comments made by Israel’s ambassador to India, calling them a “baseless characterisation”. stories.title: FO rejects US intelligence chief’s claims about Pakistan missile threat
stories.description: The Foreign Office (FO) on Thursday categorically rejected the statement by US Dire­ctor of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard over Pakistan’s missile capabilities posing a threat to the country. stories.title: Press review: Europe seeks to block US-Russia thaw as Russia eyes role in US-Iran talks
stories.description: Top stories from the Russian press on Friday, June 6th

stories.title: Israel to continue striking Beirut until Lebanon disarms Hezbollah — defense minister
stories.description: "Agreements must be honored and the Lebanese government does not do what is required, Israel will continue to act, and with great force," Israel Katz said

stories.title: Orban sees EU bracing for another decade of countering Russia amid proxy war in Ukraine
stories.description: Commenting on the Ukraine crisis, the Hungarian PM said that Brussels viewed Ukraine as "the first line of European defense" where pan-European interests were currently being defended
